component.name: Bongo Penguin
component.summary: Tux drums along with every keystroke

description.p: Bongo Penguin is a Bongo-Cat-style applet for the COSMIC desktop, starring Tux the Linux penguin. It lives in the panel or dock and flips its flippers in time with your keyboard and mouse — system-wide, including Wayland apps, native Linux games, and titles running under Proton or Wine.

ul.li: Panel and dock applet, horizontal and vertical layouts
Four penguin poses (idle, left, right, both flippers)
System-wide input capture via evdev — no focus grab required
Encrypted keystroke counter (AES-256-GCM) that survives reboots
Battery-friendly: epoll event streams, zero polling, minimal disk I/O

description.p: Requires: your user must be in the "input" group to read from /dev/input/event*. After installing, run once in a terminal:
Then log out and log back in. The applet will show an in-app reminder with this command if it starts without the required permission.

screenshot.caption: Bongo Penguin sitting in the COSMIC panel
